title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
summary: Addy Osmani outlines three core architectural patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy for efficient idle management, durable checkpointing for state persistence, and separated evaluation for robust execution. These patterns enable multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.
writeup: Addy Osmani presents three architectural patterns for building production-grade AI agents that run over extended periods: event-driven dormancy for efficient idle management, durable checkpointing for state persistence, and separated evaluation for robust execution. The framing targets multi-day workflows that go beyond stateless chatbots, where managing idle time, preserving state across long spans, and isolating evaluation become design requirements. The takeaways are concrete: dormancy keeps idle agents efficient, checkpointing persists state so work survives interruptions, and separating evaluation supports more reliable execution.
